action.title: Enjoy the Calm

Focus: Brain fog or lack of urgency.

data_risk.title: The 'Collagen Eater'

mechanisms.how: Your variant (2G allele) is 'Hyper-Active.' It eats collagen too aggressively, especially after sun exposure. You degrade your skin's structural matrix faster than you can rebuild it, leading to premature wrinkles and sagging.

Joints: Degradation of joint lining.

action.title: Sun Defense
action.body: Sunlight is the trigger that tells MMP1 to start eating. You must use Zinc-based sunscreen and wear hats. Retinol (Vitamin A) helps slow down MMP expression and forces the skin to rebuild collagen faster.

mechanisms.what: MYF5 is a transcription factor that acts as the 'Start Button' for muscle stem cells (satellite cells) to fuse with existing fibers and create growth.

mechanisms.yours: Your Result: Low Satellite Cell Recruitment.

action.title: Metabolic Accumulation
action.body: Mechanical tension isn't enough. You need 'Metabolic Stress'—high reps, short rest, and 'the pump'—to chemically force your satellite cells to activate. Eat in a 500+ calorie surplus or you will not grow.

mechanisms.what: MYF5 regulates the proliferation of muscle satellite cells, which are the biological prerequisite for hypertrophy (growth).
mechanisms.how: You are a 'High Responder.' Even moderate resistance training triggers a cascade of satellite cell activation. Your body is eager to repair micro-tears by laying down new contractile tissue. You add density and size significantly faster than the average population given the same stimulus.
mechanisms.yours: Your Result: High Satellite Cell Recruitment.

action.title: Progressive Overload

data_risk.title: The 'Antidepressant' Metabolizer

mechanisms.what: CYP2C19 metabolizes many common drugs, including Acid Reflux meds (PPIs) and Antidepressants (SSRIs).
mechanisms.how: You are a 'Poor Metabolizer.' These drugs build up in your blood to dangerous levels because you cannot break them down. A standard dose of Lexapro or Omeprazole might be an overdose for you.

mechanisms.what: Butyrylcholinesterase (BCHE) is a detox enzyme that breaks down choline esters, including succinylcholine (a muscle relaxant used in anesthesia) and certain pesticides.
mechanisms.how: You possess the 'Atypical' variant. Your enzyme works at a fraction of normal speed. A standard dose of anesthesia that should wear off in 5 minutes may paralyze your diaphragm for hours (Apnea). You are also hypersensitive to agricultural pesticides.

action.title: Medical Alert
action.body: You must inform any surgeon or anesthesiologist about your BCHE status. In your daily life, wash all produce thoroughly or buy organic to avoid organophosphate pesticide residues.

mechanisms.how: Your enzyme activity is robust. You rapidly hydrolyze toxic esters, providing a strong biological shield against environmental neurotoxins. While you shouldn't drink pesticide, your threshold for toxicity is significantly higher than the average person.

action.title: Environmental Resilience
action.body: You are naturally protected against the trace toxins found in modern food systems, though standard avoidance is still recommended for general health.

mechanisms.what: SLC2A9 encodes the GLUT9 transporter, the primary gatekeeper in the kidney that decides whether to pee out Uric Acid or put it back into the blood.
mechanisms.how: Your transporter is hyper-active. It aggressively reabsorbs uric acid back into the bloodstream. This keeps your serum levels chronically near the saturation point. You are a 'Non-Excretor.' Even small amounts of fructose or purines can spike your levels enough to cause Gout crystals to form.

action.title: Alkalize & Dilute
action.body: Hydration is physics: more water equals lower saturation. You must also limit Fructose (sugar) strictly, as fructose metabolism generates uric acid as a byproduct. Tart Cherry extract is highly effective for your genotype.

mechanisms.what: SLC2A9 maintains the balance of Uric Acid, which acts as an antioxidant at normal levels but a toxin at high levels.
mechanisms.how: Your variant creates a 'loose' gate. Your kidneys dump uric acid into the urine with exceptional efficiency. You maintain naturally low serum urate levels, making you virtually immune to dietary-induced Gout and protecting your blood vessels from urate-induced hypertension.

action.title: Dietary Freedom
action.body: While others must fear red meat and seafood, your kidneys are efficient enough to clear the purine load. You have a massive biological buffer against inflammation.

mechanisms.what: Prothrombin (Factor II) is the precursor protein that gets cleaved into Thrombin, the enzyme that turns liquid blood into a solid gel (clot).
mechanisms.how: You carry the G20210A mutation. This is a gain-of-function mutation in the mRNA, causing your liver to overproduce Prothrombin. You have 30% more clotting factor floating in your blood than normal. This turns your blood into 'sludge,' ready to clot at the slightest provocation (like sitting on a plane).

action.title: Circulation Protocol
action.body: Stasis is death. Never sit for more than 2 hours without moving. You must stay hyper-hydrated to reduce blood viscosity. Avoid Estrogen (birth control/HRT) as it thickens blood further.

mechanisms.what: The coagulation cascade is a delicate balance between bleeding and clotting
mechanisms.how: Your Prothrombin levels are perfectly regulated. You have the exact right amount of clotting factor to seal a wound instantly, but not enough to trigger random clots during travel or stress. Your vascular risk profile is baseline optimal.

action.title: Vascular Health
action.body: You have a natural protection against the clotting risks associated with aging. Maintain this by staying active and hydrated.

data_risk.title: The 'Lung Protector'

mechanisms.what: SERPINA1 produces Alpha-1 Antitrypsin, a protein that coats the lungs and protects them from inflammation and smoke damage.
mechanisms.how: Your variant (The 'S' or 'Z' allele) produces a defective protein that gets stuck in the liver and doesn't reach the lungs. Your lungs are left unshielded. Smoke or pollution causes rapid, permanent damage (Emphysema) much faster than in others.

Liver: The trapped protein can stress the liver.

action.title: Zero Smoke Tolerance
action.body: You have no biological buffer against smoke. Cigarettes, vaping, or living with a smoker are exceptionally dangerous for you. You must use HEPA air filters at home to reduce the particulate load your unshielded lungs have to handle.

data_risk.title: The 'Long-Term Memory' Gene

mechanisms.what: PRNP creates prion proteins that support long-term memory maintenance and synaptic plasticity.
mechanisms.how: Your variant (M129V) is associated with reduced cognitive stability in older age. It affects how the brain folds proteins, making you slightly more susceptible to protein-folding errors (like those seen in cognitive decline).

action.title: Autophagy for the Brain
action.body: You need to clear out misfolded proteins regularly. Sleep is when this cleaning (Glymphatic System) happens. You cannot sacrifice sleep. Time-Restricted Feeding (12+ hours) also triggers autophagy to clean up cellular debris.

data_risk.title: The 'Testosterone Finisher'

mechanisms.what: HSD17B3 converts Androstenedione into active Testosterone. It is the final step in testosterone production.
mechanisms.how: Your variant reduces the enzyme's efficiency. You may have plenty of precursor hormones, but you struggle to finish the job and create the active Testosterone molecule. This can manifest as lower vitality or difficulty building muscle.

Metabolic: Reduced insulin sensitivity due to low T.

action.title: Zinc & Resistance
action.body: This enzyme is highly Zinc-dependent. Ensure Zinc levels are optimal. Heavy resistance training (lifting weights) sends a 'demand signal' that forces the body to upregulate this enzyme to meet the need for testosterone.

data_risk.title: The 'Cortisol Clearer'

mechanisms.what: CYP3A5 helps clear cortisol (stress hormone) from the body.
mechanisms.how: You are a 'Non-Expressor' (standard in many populations). You do not produce this enzyme. You rely entirely on CYP3A4 to clear stress. This means cortisol lingers in your system longer after a stressful event. You stay 'wired' long after the threat is gone.

risks: Stress: High baseline cortisol.
Sleep: 'Tired but wired' feeling at night.
Salt: Higher salt sensitivity due to cortisol.

action.title: Active Relaxation

data_risk.title: The 'Spine & Joint' Builder

Joints: Early knee degradation.

action.body: Your spine is fragile. You must practice strict 'Spine Hygiene.' Avoid spinal flexion under load (bad deadlift form). Decompression (hanging/inversion) helps hydrate the discs. Collagen Type II (from chicken sternum) specifically targets this tissue.

data_risk.title: The 'Insulin Gate'

mechanisms.what: KCNJ11 controls the potassium channel in pancreatic beta-cells. It acts as the physical gate that must close to release insulin.
mechanisms.how: Your variant (E23K) keeps the gate open too long. This suppresses insulin secretion in response to glucose. Your pancreas is structurally hesitant to release insulin, leading to chronically higher blood sugar levels after meals.

risks: Metabolic: 40% higher risk of Type 2 Diabetes.

data_risk.title: The 'Heart Attack' Locus (9p21)

mechanisms.how: You carry the high-risk variant. Your arterial walls are prone to rapid proliferation and plaque formation. This is the single strongest genetic marker for Coronary Artery Disease (CAD) and early heart attacks, independent of cholesterol levels.

risks: Cardio: Significant risk of Coronary Artery Disease.
Vascular: Artery stiffness and aneurysm risk.

action.title: Arterial Defense

data_risk.title: The 'Cellular Rustproofer'

mechanisms.what: SOD1 is the primary antioxidant enzyme in the cytosol (the main fluid of your cells). It neutralizes Superoxide, a dangerous free radical.

risks: Brain: Oxidative damage to neurons.
Eye: Higher risk of cataracts.
General: Accelerated biological aging.

action.body: SOD1 is a Copper/Zinc dependent enzyme. If you are deficient in either, the enzyme fails. Ensure you get adequate Zinc (immune) balanced with Copper (organ meats/chocolate) to keep this rustproofer running.

mechanisms.how: Your variant creates a 'loose' anchor. Electrons leak out of the chain before they can create energy. These leaked electrons become ROS (Free Radicals), causing fatigue and damage simultaneously.

risks: Energy: Exercise intolerance and fatigue.

data_risk.title: The 'Androgen Maker'

mechanisms.how: Your variant increases enzyme activity, pushing the pathway strongly toward Androgens. While this can mean higher testosterone (good for muscle), it is often linked to PCOS in women and higher blood pressure in men.

Men: Male pattern baldness (driven by high androgens).
Cardio: Salt-sensitive hypertension.

action.body: Insulin supercharges this enzyme. If you have this variant, high sugar intake will cause massive spikes in acne and androgenic symptoms. Keeping insulin low is the most effective way to calm this pathway down.

data_risk.title: The 'Fertility Sensor'

mechanisms.what: LHCGR is the receptor for Luteinizing Hormone (LH). In women, it triggers ovulation; in men, it triggers testosterone production.
mechanisms.how: Your variant makes the receptor 'deaf' (less sensitive). The body has to scream (produce high LH) to get a response. This hormonal imbalance is a key genetic driver of PCOS and fertility struggles.

risks: Fertility: Irregular ovulation or low T production.
Hormonal: High LH/FSH ratio.

action.body: Since the receptor is weak, you must optimize the signal. Inositol (Myo-Inositol) is the second messenger that helps the LH signal get *inside* the cell. It is the gold standard supplement for this specific genetic block.

data_risk.title: The 'Gluten Sentry' (DQ8)

mechanisms.how: You carry the DQ8 risk allele. While slightly less aggressive than DQ2, it still flags gluten as a pathogen. Eating wheat keeps your immune system in a state of 'low-level war,' leading to systemic inflammation and potential autoimmunity.

risks: Gut: Celiac Disease or Non-Celiac Gluten Sensitivity.
Neuro: 'Gluten Ataxia' (dizziness/brain fog from wheat).
Skin: Dermatitis Herpetiformis (gluten rash).

action.body: You cannot train your immune system to ignore this. The sentry is genetic. The only solution is removing the trigger. A strict trial of 30 days gluten-free usually resolves unexplained fatigue and brain fog for this genotype.

data_risk.title: The 'Thyroid Receiver'

mechanisms.what: THRB is the receptor that catches T3 hormone and turns on your metabolism.
mechanisms.how: Your variant has reduced sensitivity. You can have plenty of thyroid hormone in your blood, but your cells don't 'hear' it. You may have all the symptoms of hypothyroidism (fatigue, weight gain) despite having 'perfect' blood test results.

action.body: Zinc is required for the thyroid receptor to attach to DNA. High-dose Zinc (30mg) can sometimes improve receptor sensitivity. You also need to be aggressive about removing receptor blockers like Chlorine and Fluoride (water filters).

data_risk.title: The 'Power Recycler'

mechanisms.what: Creatine Kinase (CKM) recycles ATP energy during explosive movements (sprinting, lifting). It is your 10-second energy battery.
mechanisms.how: Your variant reduces the efficiency of this recycling. Your 'battery' drains faster during high-intensity bursts. You fatigue quickly during heavy lifting or sprints and take longer to recover between sets.

risks: Fitness: Poor performance in CrossFit or HIIT.
Muscle: Higher levels of muscle damage markers post-workout.

action.title: Load the Battery
action.body: Since the recycling machine is slow, you must make the battery bigger. Creatine Monohydrate (5g daily) is non-negotiable for you. It saturates the muscle with raw fuel, masking the inefficiency of the enzyme.

mechanisms.what: The Vitamin D Receptor (VDR) is not just a passive dock; it is a nuclear transcription factor. It must bind to Vitamin D, enter the nucleus, and unlock DNA to turn on 900+ genes involved in immunity and bone repair.
mechanisms.how: Your receptor (likely Taq1 or Bsm1 variant) has a shape change that reduces its binding efficiency. Even with normal Vitamin D levels in your blood, the 'key' turns the lock very slowly. This results in weak gene expression, leaving your immune system sluggish and your bones less able to mineralize.

mechanisms.how: Your receptor is hypersensitive. It creates a robust signal cascade even with moderate sun exposure. action.body: Your robust VDR signaling provides a powerful baseline defense. Moderate sun exposure is likely all you need to maintain a super-charged immune response.

action.title: The Walking Cure
action.body: Since your pancreas is slow, you must use your muscles to clear the sugar. A 15-minute walk immediately after eating engages the GLUT4 transporters in your legs, sucking glucose out of the blood without needing high insulin levels. This saves your pancreas from burnout.

mechanisms.how: Your genotype allows for a sharp, precise insulin response. You clear blood sugar spikes rapidly, preventing the glycation damage that ages skin and organs. action.body: You are metabolically flexible. You can utilize carbohydrates for high-intensity athletic recovery far more effectively than the average person. Post-workout carbs will be driven directly into muscle tissue rather than lingering in the blood.

action.body: You have to build the shield yourself. 'Hormesis' (good stress) turns on the repair pathways that your genetics didn't activate automatically. Sauna usage (Heat Shock Proteins) is the best mimetic for this genetic effect.

action.title: Intensity Matters

action.body: You must provide the raw materials to force the vessels open. Consume high-nitrate foods like Beets, Arugula, and Spinach. Supplementing with L-Citrulline or Agmatine can also bypass the enzymatic bottleneck to restore blood flow.

This is the genetic signature of elite endurance athletes and those with naturally healthy blood pressure.
mechanisms.yours: Your Result: High Nitric Oxide Production.

action.body: You are built for output. Your cardiovascular system is highly efficient. Activities like running, cycling, or high-rep training suit your physiology perfectly.

action.body: Always start with half the recommended dose for new supplements or medications (under doctor supervision). Your body needs more time to clear chemicals. Avoid Grapefruit juice, which slows this enzyme even further.

mechanisms.what: CYP3A4 acts as the primary incinerator for foreign chemicals entering the body.
mechanisms.how: Your liver is a fortress. You metabolize drugs and toxins with exceptional speed. This prevents accumulation and toxicity. You can handle caffeine, environmental exposures, and medications with minimal side effects.

action.title: Resilience
action.body: You are the 'Canary in the Coal Mine' in reverse—you survive while others get sick. Just remember: fast metabolism means some meds might wear off faster than expected.

mechanisms.what: Insulin Receptor Substrate 1 (IRS1) is the 'doorman' that opens the cell to let glucose in when Insulin knocks.
mechanisms.how: Your doorman is hard of hearing. Your cells resist the insulin signal, leaving glucose floating in the blood where it causes glycation (aging) damage. You require 2-3x more insulin to get the same amount of sugar into the muscle.

action.title: The Muscle Sink
action.body: Muscle contraction opens a 'back door' for glucose (GLUT4) that doesn't require insulin. You must use this backdoor. Post-meal walking or squats are essential to manually clear blood sugar.

mechanisms.what: IRS1 dictates whether calories are sent to muscle cells (for repair) or fat cells (for storage).
mechanisms.how: Your receptors are hypersensitive. A tiny amount of insulin opens the floodgates to your muscles. You rapidly soak up nutrients after a meal, driving recovery and keeping blood sugar stable. You are the person who builds muscle easily and stays lean.

action.title: Carb Utilization
action.body: Carbohydrates are a performance tool for you. Timing them around workouts will yield massive anabolic benefits with very little fat gain.

mechanisms.what: SIRT1 is known as the 'Longevity Gene.' It scans your DNA for breaks and metabolic damage, coordinating repairs and silencing genes that cause aging.
mechanisms.how: Your SIRT1 expression is naturally lower. Think of it as a repair crew that is understaffed. Biological damage (from sun, sugar, stress) accumulates faster because the cleanup crew can't keep up with the mess. This predisposes you to accelerated metabolic aging.

action.title: Manual Activation (Hormesis)
action.body: You must wake the crew up. SIRT1 is activated by 'perceived scarcity.' Intermittent Fasting (16+ hours) and Cold Exposure (Ice Baths) are non-negotiable for you. These stressors trick your body into survival mode, forcing SIRT1 to upregulate and repair the system.

mechanisms.what: SIRT1 is the commander of your cellular defense, regulating DNA repair, inflammation, and apoptosis (cell death).
mechanisms.how: You have a genetic upregulation of this pathway. Your repair crews are working overtime, 24/7. Your body detects and fixes DNA breaks with incredible efficiency, keeping your 'biological age' younger than your chronological age. You are naturally resistant to the metabolic wear-and-tear of modern life.

action.title: Compound Interest
action.body: You have a winning lottery ticket; don't spend it all. If you combine this genetic advantage with fasting and clean living, you have the potential for exceptional longevity. Avoid 'spending' your advantage on smoking or excessive sugar.

mechanisms.what: Mitochondria produce energy by pumping protons across a membrane to spin a turbine. UCP2 is a valve that usually lets protons skip the turbine to generate heat.
mechanisms.how: Your valve is sealed tight. Your mitochondria trap every single proton to make ATP (energy). While efficient for famine survival, today it means you store excess calories as fat rather than burning them as body heat.

action.title: Cold Activation
action.body: You must manually force the valves open. Cold Thermogenesis (Ice Baths) forces the body to activate UCP1/UCP2 to prevent hypothermia, burning fat for heat.

mechanisms.what: UCP2 acts as an 'Uncoupling Protein' in the inner mitochondrial membrane.
mechanisms.how: Your mitochondria are naturally 'leaky.' Instead of converting 100% of fuel into chemical energy (ATP), your cells allow a significant portion of protons to flow back into the matrix, releasing the energy as pure heat. This raises your Basal Metabolic Rate and protects against obesity.

action.title: Carb Resilience
action.body: You can handle caloric surpluses better than most. Your body naturally upregulates heat production after large meals to dissipate the excess energy.

mechanisms.what: Apolipoprotein E (APOE) is the primary cholesterol transporter in the brain and nervous system. It creates the 'boats' that ferry lipids to neurons for repair and clears amyloid-beta debris.
mechanisms.how: You carry the E4 allele, which alters the structure of the transporter. Your 'boats' are fragile and prone to breaking down, dumping cholesterol and inflammation into the brain tissue. This creates a 'pro-inflammatory' environment that struggles to clear plaques, significantly raising the risk of neurodegeneration if saturated fat intake is high.

action.title: Lipid Restriction Strategy
action.body: Your brain cannot handle Saturated Fats (Butter, Coconut Oil, Fatty Beef). They act as rocket fuel for inflammation. You must switch to Monounsaturated Fats (Olive Oil, Avocado) and high-dose DHA (Fish Oil) to manually support neuronal repair.

mechanisms.what: APOE is the master regulator of lipid transport and brain plaque clearance.
mechanisms.how: You carry the neutral (E3) or protective (E2) variant. Your lipid transporters are robust and efficient. They rapidly clear amyloid debris and recycle cholesterol without triggering inflammation. This provides a potent biological buffer against cognitive decline and allows for greater dietary flexibility with fats.

action.title: Metabolic Flexibility
action.body: You have a natural advantage in brain health. To maximize it, focus on 'Brain Fuels' like Ketones or MCTs, which your efficient transporters can utilize effectively.

mechanisms.what: ALDH2 is the liver enzyme responsible for converting Acetaldehyde (a toxic carcinogen) into Acetate (harmless vinegar).
mechanisms.how: Your enzyme is structurally defective. It works at <10% efficiency. When you drink alcohol or breathe city smog, Acetaldehyde accumulates instantly in your blood. The 'flush' you feel is actually a toxic immunological reaction. This causes DNA damage at a rate 10x higher than normal.

action.title: Cysteine Support
action.body: If you cannot avoid exposure, you must supplement with NAC (N-Acetyl Cysteine). It provides the precursor for Glutathione, which acts as a 'backup' detox pathway since your primary ALDH2 pathway is broken.

mechanisms.what: ALDH2 is the rapid-response unit for neutralizing environmental aldehydes.
mechanisms.how: Your enzyme works at hyper-speed. You convert toxins into harmless energy almost instantly. This protects your DNA from the mutation risks associated with alcohol, smoke, and exhaust fumes. You have a 'cast-iron' tolerance for environmental stressors.

action.title: Toxin Resistance
action.body: You are biologically equipped for modern urban living, but remember that rapid detox doesn't make alcohol healthy—it just makes it less immediately damaging.

mechanisms.what: Brain-Derived Neurotrophic Factor (BDNF) is a protein that acts as 'fertilizer' for your neurons, encouraging the growth of new synapses and the repair of damaged ones.
mechanisms.how: You carry the 'Met' allele, which affects the secretion of BDNF. Your brain releases this growth factor more reluctantly. This doesn't mean you can't learn, but it means 'neuro-friction' is higher for you. You are more prone to getting stuck in emotional ruts or finding it difficult to unlearn bad habits.

action.title: High-Intensity Trigger
action.body: You cannot rely on passive learning. You need a powerful physiological trigger to force BDNF release. High-Intensity Interval Training (HIIT) is the most potent tool you have. Sprinting or heavy lifting alerts your biology that adaptation is necessary, flooding the brain with the growth factor you lack.

mechanisms.what: Brain-Derived Neurotrophic Factor (BDNF) is the master molecule of learning, memory, and neural adaptation.
mechanisms.how: You are a 'Val/Val' genotype, meaning your brain is bathed in BDNF. Your neural networks are incredibly malleable. You can wire in new skills (instruments, languages, complex movements) at a speed that baffles others. You adapt to trauma and stress rapidly because your brain physically rewires itself around the damage.

action.title: Skill Stacking
action.body: Your brain craves complexity. Boredom is your enemy. You should be learning two distinct skills at once (e.g., coding and martial arts) to utilize this capacity. Your brain is a Ferrari; don't drive it in a school zone.

mechanisms.what: Glutathione S-Transferase P1 (GSTP1) is the enzyme that conjugates (tags) toxins and free radicals with glutathione so they can be excreted.
mechanisms.how: Your tagging gun is jammed (Ile105Val variant). Free radicals from exhaust, smoke, or intense exercise pile up faster than you can tag them. This leads to prolonged inflammation, delayed recovery, and susceptibility to respiratory issues.

action.title: Sulforaphane Activation
action.body: You need to upregulate other Phase 2 enzymes to compensate. Sulforaphane (found in Broccoli Sprouts) is the most potent natural switch to turn on your backup detox systems.

mechanisms.what: GSTP1 provides the primary defense against oxidative damage in lung and muscle tissue.
mechanisms.how: Your enzymes work at peak efficiency. You neutralize oxidative threats instantly. This allows you to train harder, recover faster, and breathe polluted air with significantly less cellular damage than the average person.

action.title: Metabolic Durability
action.body: You have a high ceiling for physical stress. You can push intensity in your workouts without suffering the lingering inflammation that slows others down.

mechanisms.what: The Vitamin D Receptor (VDR) is not just a passive dock; it is a nuclear transcription factor. It must bind to Vitamin D, enter the nucleus, and unlock DNA to turn on 900+ genes involved in immunity and bone repair.
mechanisms.how: Your receptor (likely Taq1 or Bsm1 variant) has a shape change that reduces its binding efficiency. Even with normal Vitamin D levels in your blood, the 'key' turns the lock very slowly. This results in weak gene expression, leaving your immune system sluggish and your bones less able to mineralize.

action.title: Force the Lock
action.body: Standard dosing is not enough. You need to flood the system. You likely need blood levels of 60-80 ng/mL (higher than the standard 30) to achieve the same genetic activation as a normal person. Take Vitamin D3 with K2 and a fatty meal (Avocado) to maximize absorption.

mechanisms.what: VDR forms a complex with RXR (Retinoid X Receptor) to activate the 'Genome Defense' program.
mechanisms.how: Your receptor is hypersensitive. It creates a robust signal cascade even with moderate sun exposure. You aggressively produce cathelicidins (natural antibiotics) and absorb calcium efficiently. This gives you a 'Teflon' immune system that repels respiratory viruses and maintains bone density with ease.

action.title: Immune Shield
action.body: Your robust VDR signaling provides a powerful baseline defense. Moderate sun exposure is likely all you need to maintain a super-charged immune response.

mechanisms.what: COMT is the enzyme responsible for breaking down catecholamines (Dopamine, Adrenaline, Norepinephrine) in the prefrontal cortex.
mechanisms.how: Your enzyme works slowly. Dopamine lingers in your brain. This is a double-edged sword: You have incredible attention to detail, high IQ potential, and complex planning ability. But under stress, that lingering chemistry turns into anxiety, looping thoughts, and an inability to 'let it go.'

action.title: Tactical Decompression
action.body: You cannot just 'calm down' instantly; your chemistry prohibits it. It takes you 20+ minutes to clear adrenaline. When stressed, you must physically remove yourself from the stimulus. Magnesium and L-Theanine are your chemical allies to help speed up the clearance.

mechanisms.what: COMT cleans the brain of stress neurochemicals, resetting your baseline for the next challenge.
mechanisms.how: You are a 'Val/Val' genotype. Your enzymes are aggressive incinerators of stress. In high-pressure situations (public speaking, emergencies), while others freeze, your brain clears the fog instantly. You are biologically engineered for chaos and high-stakes performance.

action.title: Seek Friction
action.body: A quiet life is torture for you. You metabolize dopamine so fast that you can feel unmotivated in low-stress environments. You need deadlines, competition, and risk to feel fully alive and cognitively engaged.
